coredata.dc:title: Prediction of Li-ion battery state of health based on data-driven algorithm
coredata.prism:copyright: © 2022 The Author(s). Published by Elsevier Ltd.
coredata.prism:publisher: The Author(s). Published by Elsevier Ltd.
coredata.prism:issueName: 2022 International Conference on the Energy Internet and Energy Interactive Technology
coredata.dc:creator: Sun, Hanlei
Yang, Dongfang
Du, Jiaxuan
Li, Ping
Wang, Kai
coredata.dc:description: Li-ion battery state of health (SOH) is a key parameter for characterizing actual battery life. SOH cannot be measured directly. In order to further improve the accuracy of Li-ion battery SOH estimation, a combined model based on health feature parameters combined with EMD-ICA-GRU is proposed to predict the SOH of Li-ion batteries. The capacity regeneration phenomenon and data noise are decomposed by empirical mode decomposition (EMD), and then the SOH-related health indicators are deeply mined using incremental capacity analysis (ICA), and the peaks of IC curves and their corresponding voltages are extracted as the input of the model. Then, gated recurrent units (GRUs) are formed into a combined SOH estimation model by adaptive weighting factors. Finally, it is validated against the NASA lithium battery dataset. Experimental results show that the mean squared error (MSE) of the proposed combined model can reach about 0.3%, and it has stronger generalization and prediction accuracy than other algorithms driven by independent estimation data.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Lithium-ion battery
SOH
ICA
EMD
GRU

MSE Comparison Chart of Various Neural Network Methods.
Comparison of multi-model test set prediction results.

ce:dochead.ce:textfn: International Conference on the Energy Internet and Energy Interactive Technology, South China University of Technology, 2022, 25–27 March 2022, Wuhan, China

head.ce:title: Prediction of Li-ion battery state of health based on data-driven algorithm

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Electrical Engineering, Weihai Innovation Research Institute, Qingdao University, Qingdao, 266000, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: School of Electrical Engineering, Weihai Innovation Research Institute, Qingdao University

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Xi’an Traffic Engineering Institute, Xi’an, 710300, China
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Xi’an Traffic Engineering Institute, Xi’an, 710300, China

ce:affiliation.ce:label: c
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Northeast Electric Power University, Ji’lin, 132012, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Dongying District Science and Technology Bureau

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Dongying District Science and Technology Bureau, Dong’ying, 257073, China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Li-ion battery state of health (SOH) is a key parameter for characterizing actual battery life. SOH cannot be measured directly. In order to further improve the accuracy of Li-ion battery SOH estimation, a combined model based on health feature parameters combined with EMD-ICA-GRU is proposed to predict the SOH of Li-ion batteries. The capacity regeneration phenomenon and data noise are decomposed by empirical mode decomposition (EMD), and then the SOH-related health indicators are deeply mined using incremental capacity analysis (ICA), and the peaks of IC curves and their corresponding voltages are extracted as the input of the model. Then, gated recurrent units (GRUs) are formed into a combined SOH estimation model by adaptive weighting factors. Finally, it is validated against the NASA lithium battery dataset. Experimental results show that the mean squared error (MSE) of the proposed combined model can reach about 0.3%, and it has stronger generalization and prediction accuracy than other algorithms driven by independent estimation data.

ce:data-availability.ce:para: The data that has been used is confidential.

ce:section.ce:para: With the high rate of development of new energy technologies, Li ion batteries ushered in a huge development in this wave, especially in the field of new energy vehicles, and electric vehicles powered by Li ion batteries are gradually replacing conventional fuel vehicles [1–3]. One of the most central links is the battery management system (BMS), and the precise estimation of state of Health (soh) is one of the important bases of BMS function [4,5], and SOH as an important factor to measure battery performance, researchers pay much attention to it in the development and application of Li ion battery technology [6]. With an increase in the number of batteries used, excessive charging and discharging of batteries, improper use army may lead to the reduction of battery life with aging [7]. Therefore, precise detection of SOH allows technicians to adjust the charge discharge strategy of the battery pack in a timely manner [8], repair or replace the aged battery monomers, and avoid uncontrollable crisis events during the application of lithium-ion batteries, which is an important guarantee for the safe and reliable use of EVs [9].

In recent years, a wide range of SOH estimation methods have been developed. For example, Ge et al. used an improved Extreme Learning Machine (ELM) combined with the Bat Algorithm (BA) to predict the SOH of lithium-ion batteries. Li et al. estimated SOH using a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) neural network with practical feature input. Sun et al. proposed a battery aging evaluation method based on ICA and double-layer LSTM.

This study proposes a novel battery aging assessment method for GRU, combining ICA as the input feature extraction method and EMD as the data regeneration and fluctuation elimination method. In addition, various stressors are considered in our model. Typically, researchers apply traditional data and validation methods, such as K-fold verification, in contrast to our validation of our model on an untrained test scenario.

The model uses a data-driven algorithm that eliminates the need to model the internal principles of lithium-ion batteries, and only needs to obtain characteristic indicators related to health. In data processing, EMD is used to filter noise and remove the regeneration phenomenon, and ICA is used to extract features, which improves the interpretability and high correlation of features. The multi-model combined prediction model is constructed by adaptive weights to achieve accurate prediction of lithium battery SOH.

The dataset used in this study was obtained from NASA lab, which were b0005, b0006, b0007 and B0018 four batteries, respectively, and the reason for selection was that these four batteries were all observed at 24 °C to observe the data changes of aging of the batteries, which specifically included the data of voltage, current, temperature, internal resistance, and so on, with the voltage charge and discharge variation ranging from 2.7 V to 4.2 V, and the charging process mainly started by charging in constant current mode (CC) with 1.5 A, And reaching a constant voltage charge mode (CV) at voltage to 4.2 V and progressively decreasing the charge current to around 20 mA to stop charging (see Fig. 1).

EMD is an efficient signal processing technique which can extract low-frequency and high-frequency components from dynamic signals. As shown in Fig. 2, B0005, B0006, B0007 and B0018 are the four sets of data used for training in this paper. During the process of cyclic charging and discharging, capacity regeneration and more local fluctuations occur during the attenuation of battery capacity. EMD can decompose the non-stationary data set into residual series and several intrinsic mode functions (IMFs) by screening the data.

For the specific implementation of EMD, please refer to [10] for details. The regeneration phenomenon and local fluctuation are high-frequency signals, the derived form are the IMF functions. The monotonic trend of capacity degradation is the low-frequency signal, and the derived form is the residual sequence. In this study, we chose to retain the residual sequence and use the model to fit the impact of the IMF function, so that AI can fully understand the whole process of lithium-ion battery capacity decline. The EMD decomposition results are shown in Fig. 3 by taking the B0005 dataset as an example.

ICA is a method to deeply mine and analyze the relationship between voltage and capacity during charging and discharging of lithium-ion batteries. During the charging process of the sub-lithium-ion battery, a voltage plateau will appear during the voltage rise, and the voltage changes slowly during this period. This is due to the relative equilibrium state caused by the chemical reactions inside the lithium-ion battery. For detailed calculation analysis, please refer to [11]. The derived IC curve is filtered as shown in Fig. 4. It can be seen that as the number of cycles increases, the peak value of the IC curves gradually decreases, and the peak position gradually moves to the right. As the number of cycles increases, the internal resistance of the battery increases, resulting in a change in the peak value of the IC curve. It can reflect the decline of its battery health and is a good health indicator.

Therefore, this study extracted two relevant features from the IC curve, which are the peak amplitude and the peak corresponding voltage of the IC curve [12]. The peak we selected was the highest one, and it was extracted using the Scipy peak-finding algorithm [13]. These two features were then quantified with the person correlation coefficient, where the peak amplitude as a health indicator had a correlation coefficient of 0.984 with the SOH of Li-ion batteries, and the correlation coefficient of the peak voltage with the SOH was −0.972. are strongly correlated.

As an improved form of recurrent neural network, which has the characteristics of fast computation rate, can improve the gradient explosion in the neural network as well as long-term dependence problems, can effectively mine the characteristics in the timing data, and cooperate with dropout method has a good robustness [14].

As shown in Fig. 5 (a), the input versus output of the GRU module consists of two parts, the input including the current input
x
t
and the state component output from the previous cycle node
h
t − 1
, this state component contains data information from the previous node, and the current output after GRU layer fitting includes the current output
y
t
and the state component passed to the next node
h
t
.

Fig. 5(b) shows the internal structure of the GRU. Its internal calculation process can be divided into two gated loops, reset gate and update gate [15]. The mathematical formula is:
(1)
z
t
= σ
W
z
⋅
h
t − 1
,
x
t
(2)
r
t
= σ
W
r
⋅
h
t − 1
,
x
t
(3)
h
t
′
= t a n h
W ⋅
r
t
∗
h
t − 1
,
x
t
(4)
h
t
=
1 −
z
t
∗
h
t − 1
+
z
t
∗
h
t
′

Among them,
r
t
controls the reset gate,
z
t
controls the update gate, W is the weight, and σ is the sigmoid function, which transforms the data into a value in the range of 0–1 and acts as a gate control signal. After obtaining the gate control signal, the signal passes through the reset gate to get
h
t − 1
, then combine
h
t − 1
with the input
x
t
, and get
h
t
′
by tanh activation function, at last the memory is updated through formula (4).

Compared with LSTM, GRU has one less gating unit and fewer parameters than LSTM [16]. It can also effectively process data with sequence characteristics, mine time series information from it, and use gating mechanism to improve long-term dependency problems, it is an excellent algorithm that takes into account both efficiency and accuracy.

The overall estimation process of SOH is shown in Fig. 6. Firstly, we need extract and analyze the NASA lithium-ion battery data set, and use EMD to process the phenomenon of capacity regeneration and noise fluctuation in the data; at the same time, Normalize the data and extract the IC curve for incremental capacity analysis. The correlation coefficient is calculated to extract health indicators. Then, the health index and capacity are put into the GRU model as the input and output of the neural network respectively, and the output results are added through the adaptive weight module to realize the estimation of the SOH of the lithium-ion battery.

In order to verify the estimation accuracy of the above-mentioned Li-ion battery SOH joint estimation model, the B5 battery data is used as the main parameter [17]. The B5 battery has a total of 166 valid cycles, and the first 100 cycles of the battery will be used as the training set. The last 66 times are used as the validation set. The learning rate of the GRU neural network is set to 0.001, the time step is 1, the data dimension is 4, the number of hidden layer neurons is 64, and the maximum number of trainings is 200 [18]. The dropout layer is added to the GRU layer as a regularization method with a parameter of 0.2 [19–21] to improve the generalization ability of the network model.

In this study, we use python as the simulation language, use BP neural network, simple RNN, LSTM to compare with the joint estimation model proposed in this paper for prediction, LSTM and RNN both adopt the same parameters as the above GRU model, and other parameters of BP are also the same as the above, except that the regularization method combining L1 and L2 is adopted. The loss function of the model takes the MSE of the predicted SOH and the true value, and the mathematical formula is shown in (5):
(5)
M S E =
1
m
∑
i = 1
m
y
i
−
y
i
′
2

It can be seen from Figs. 7 and 8 that the EMD-ICA-GRU model proposed in this paper fits the phenomenon of capacity regeneration very well due to the excellent ability of EMD to capture high-frequency fluctuation components, which also benefits from GRU’s efficient time series information mining ability. Correspondingly, the fitting noise of other models is not always large, LSTM solves the long-term capacity dependence problem to a certain extent, and can basically and accurately complete the entire estimation process of lithium-ion battery SOH., Although RNN and BP also have a certain prediction accuracy, they are much worse than the combined model proposed in this paper. At the same time, the generalization performance of EMD-ICA-GRU model is also quite good, and the prediction accuracy in the prediction of four groups of batteries is very good. Table 1 shows the performance of different models in different battery data sets in detail. It can be found that compared with the prediction accuracy of the other three models, the EMD-ICA-GRU model shows a very good prediction accuracy, reaching an average MSE error level of about 0.3%, and the prediction performance improved by more than 50% compared with other models.

This paper focuses on developing a data-driven approach to achieve SOH prediction of Li-ion batteries with uncertainty quantification through ICA capture of practical features and regeneration phenomenon capture of EMD. The computing environment is python 3.6, the amount of computation required to implement GRU is small, and one round of training time is within 2s. In fact, developing a data-driven model that is sufficiently accurate and stable, and the various uncertainties it brings, is an open research question [22,23]. Although the proposed framework can consider both capacity long-term dependencies and prediction accuracy, the corresponding performance is highly dependent on the form of the data and the quality of the test experiments, a common problem for purely data-driven solutions.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was supported by the Youth Fund of Shandong Natural Science Foundation, China (No. ZR2020QE212), Key Projects of Shandong Natural Science Foundation, China (No. ZR2020KF020).

sb:title.sb:maintitle: A method for estimating the aging state of lithium-ion batteries based on a multi-linear integrated model

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Hanlei Sun, Dongfang Yang, Licheng Wang, Kai Wang. A method for estimating the aging state of lithium-ion batteries based on a multi-linear integrated model. International Journal of Energy Research, 0 (2022) 0-0. DOI: 10.1002/ER.8709

sb:title.sb:maintitle: A state-of-health estimation method considering capacity recovery of lithium batteries

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Yu Guo, Peng Yu, Chao Zhu, Kun Zhao, Licheng Wang, Kai Wang. A state-of-health estimation method considering capacity recovery of lithium batteries. International Journal of Energy Reseach, 0(2022)0-0 DOI: 10.1002/ER.8671

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Electrochemical impedance spectroscopy based on the state of health estimation for lithium-ion batteries

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Dezhi Li Dongfang Yang; Liwei Li; Licheng Wang; Kai Wang. Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy Based on the State of Health Estimation for Lithium-Ion Batteries. Energies 15(2022) 6665.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: A hybrid neural network model with improved input for state of charge estimation of lithium-ion battery at low temperatures

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Zhenhua Cui, Le Kang, Liwei Li, Licheng Wang, Kai Wang. A hybrid neural network model with improved input for state of charge estimation of lithium-ion battery at low temperatures. Renewable Energy 98(2022) 1328-1340.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: A combined state-of-charge estimation method for lithium-ion battery using an improved BGRU network and UKF

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Zhenhua Cui, Le Kang, Liwei Li, Licheng Wang, Kai Wang. A combined state-of-charge estimation method for lithium-ion battery using an improved BGRU network and UKF. Energy 259(2022) 124933.

Hua Yu, Wang Na, and Keyu Zhao. Simultaneous Unknown Input and State Estimation for the Linear System with a Rank-Deficient Distribution Matrix. Mathematical Problems in Engineering 2021(2021) 6693690.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: A data-driven approach with uncertainty quantification for predicting future capacities and remaining useful life of lithium-ion battery

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Kailong Liu, Yunlong Shang, Ouyang Quan, and Widanage Widanalage Dhammika. A Data-Driven Approach With Uncertainty Quantification for Predicting Future Capacities and Remaining Useful Life of Lithium-ion Battery. IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics 68 (4)(2021) 3170-3180.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: State-of-health estimation for lithium-ion batteries by combining model-based incremental capacity analysis with support vector regression

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Yajun Zhang, Yajie Liu, Jia Wang, and Tao Zhang. State-of-health estimation for lithium-ion batteries by combining model-based incremental capacity analysis with support vector regression. Energy 239(2022) 121986.
